steps.title: Identify the contrast goal
steps.content: The student's goal is not just to mention the Royal Alcázar or its tilework. The goal is to contrast two tile styles, so the answer must include both majolica and arista and show how their techniques differ.

steps.title: Use the defining details
steps.content: The notes give one key difference: majolica designs are painted directly on ceramic tiles, while arista designs are stamped into ceramic tiles. A sentence that simply says both styles appear in the palace does not accomplish the contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate by purpose
steps.content: Choice A only says both styles can be found at the palace. Choice B adds that the palace is famous for tilework, but it still does not explain any difference between the styles. Choice D describes only majolica tiles and leaves out the arista comparison. Choice C uses "whereas" to place the stamped arista technique beside the painted majolica technique, directly fulfilling the goal. The answer is C.
